summary: Package ships browser exploit that exfiltrates pendo.io session data to a webhook.site collector
details: The main entry index.js is a working exploit kit, not a library. It creates a hidden iframe pointed at pendo.io, sends a builder.io 'builder.patchUpdates' postMessage whose payload replaces /bindings/show with a script that calls fetch('https://novus-api.pendo.io/pendo/app', {credentials:'include'}), base64-encodes the response, chunks it, and exfiltrates each chunk to https://webhook.site/d4d74967-cd7f-4b43-86f0-944e71ac7c97 via Image() beacons. index.js iterates 40 hardcoded builder.io content IDs to inject the patch, and a.html is a user-facing trigger that opens pendo.io in a first-party window and navigates to qwik-playground.builder.io with a base64 XSS payload. A developer who installs this package and bundles index.js into a frontend application ships working session-cookie exfiltration code that targets their own users and third-party SaaS (Builder.io, Pendo); the hardcoded webhook.site collector is attacker-controlled regardless of the 'security research PoC' framing. The package is published on the public npm registry with no access control on who can `npm install` it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
